narrative:
The market regime held steady in Business as Usual throughout the week, though the stress score drifted lower from 24 down to 18 by Thursday close, suggesting a mild decompression of underlying tension. The decline was gradual and orderly rather than sharp, with no abrupt shocks or regime breaks. This gentle easing reflects a market that remains constructive but is slowly shedding some of the defensive positioning that had built up earlier.

High-yield spreads tightened modestly across the five-day window, compressing from 283bp on May 19 to 279bp by May 21, tracking the benign regime tone. Credit conditions have reinforced the equities narrative without contradiction, confirming that any rally has broad-based support rather than being driven by equity technicals alone. Volatility patterns aligned with this picture of contained stress, with no signs of VIX spiking or widening credit conditions despite the week's moves.

Deep value signals emerged on May 19 with BlackRock flagged as a wait candidate, followed by NextEra Energy similarly marked on May 18, though neither name developed into a conviction trade during the week. Both signals sat quietly without triggering sharp repricing or generating notable dislocation, suggesting the market was methodical rather than hunting for bargains. Watch next week for any catalyst that might reaccelerate the stress score upward or, conversely, allow the regime to complete its compression toward genuine all-clear territory.
